report_markdown:
## Week of May 4, 2026

Your calendar was empty all week. Zero meetings, zero commitments, zero scheduled obligations — a completely clear week. And you completed zero tasks. That's the full picture.

**Avoidance loop (2 weeks and counting)**

Three tasks have been overdue since April 12–13. Todoist reminded you about them on May 8, May 9, and May 10. You had 14 active tasks and didn't close a single one. The week before this — April 7 — you had 14 hours of meetings, 6 back-to-back blocks, and an excuse. This week you had nothing on your calendar and still didn't move. That tells you the meetings weren't the blocker. The loop is the blocker.

**Complete communication shutdown**

You received 112 emails and sent zero. Eighty-eight of them are sitting unread — 78% of your inbox. You have 8 threads waiting for a reply; 6 are apartment inquiries from properties actively trying to show you homes. You're clearly in a housing search. The outreach is stacking up. Summit at Madison Park, City Square Bellevue, Alt Capitol Hill, Werner, Skye Bell Town, Centerview — none answered, some waiting weeks. Gmail flagged that your storage is at 1% remaining. This isn't a full inbox because you're busy. You had the entire week open.

**Midnight delivery, every night**

7 food delivery orders in 4 days. Dairy Queen at 2:28 AM Friday. Signature Grill at 12:18 AM Saturday, then another DQ at 12:50 AM when it went wrong. Popeyes at 11:42 PM Saturday. You're not cooking. You're not leaving. You're ordering food in the middle of the night. The prior week you were sending emails after 9 PM; this week it's 2 AM food orders. The pattern of not switching off is consistent — what changed is the output.

**The one honest thing**

You have one unresolved decision driving all of this: where you're going to live. Six places are waiting for your reply. You haven't answered any of them. Everything else — the tasks not started, the emails not sent, the week that went blank — is orbiting that one thing you haven't been able to decide.

The root cause: an unresolved housing decision is keeping everything else in suspension while late-night food delivery and an empty calendar quietly confirm the paralysis.
root_cause: An unresolved high-stakes housing decision is the anchor holding everything in avoidance — the empty calendar, 0% task completion, and complete communication shutdown all orbit the same unanswered question.
